item.title: Oscillatory Neck Propagation in Polymer Films
item.description: Imagine stretching a long strip of PET, often the plastic deformation is violent, and the sample just breaks. However, sometimes the film can be stretched for more than 2 times in length, and the stress actually oscillates. The obvious manifestation is a pattern consists of opaque and translucent stripes, shown as the gif on the right. This region is what we called “neck”, which has smaller width and thickness than

item.title: Personal Static Website
item.description: Unlike dynamic sites, static websites are easier to build and maintain. In construction, one uses MarkDown files for pages, and YAML for configuration files. Some of the codes require HTML, e.g. adjusting the images. Hugo will generates all the files into HTML for publish. After construction, one needs deployment to publish, either by GitHub page, or services like Netlify. Static websites would be troublesome in updating, or realising some functions

item.title: Restricted Three-Body Systems
item.description: The three-body problem is one of the most well-known problems in classic mechanics, which has no closed form solutions. However, when we assume that the third body is much smaller in mass than the other stars, it would be much easier for us to investigate more properties including the trajectory. This is a tiny project when just graduating from high school. During the project we analysed the forbidden region and

item.title: About
item.description: Greetings!       I am now a undergraduate student majoring       Mathematics and Statistics in Keble College,       University of Oxford.       Currently I'm in the third year of my four-year master's       programme. At the moment I'm exploring my interests       for my future study through projects. My interests so       far lies

item.title: Contact
item.description: Should you have any issues, please do not hesitate to contact me.
